title: Tapping for the Urge to Fix It All This Week
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the body that wants the change to happen faster than it can
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the urge to fix everything with the body in one burst. The urge is real; the problem is the pace it sets. The script addresses the pressure to overhaul rather than move steadily.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 2 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you wake up in fix-it mode, when Monday feels like the start of a new strict regime, or when the urge to overhaul is bigger than the actual plan.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the urge to fix the body this week. The urge comes up often at the start of a new week or after a day that felt like a failure. The script works on the pressure behind the urge rather than the urge itself.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I want to fix all of this this week, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the pressure to change it all fast is here again, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though slow feels like failure right now, I honour where I am, and I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I want this sorted this week.
**Side of eye:** The pressure is loud this morning.
**Under eye:** I want the big change and I want it fast.
**Under nose:** Slow feels like it does not count.
**Chin:** I am tired of taking it week by week.
**Collarbone:** The all-or-nothing part of me wants to take over.
**Under arm:** I have been here before and pushed too hard.
**Top of head:** The urge to fix it all this week is back.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I can move steadily this week instead.
**Side of eye:** Steady counts. Steady adds up.
**Under eye:** Slow is a real path, not a delay.
**Under nose:** I do not have to fix everything today.
**Chin:** One steady week matters more than one overhaul week.
**Collarbone:** I can choose the pace that I can keep.
**Under arm:** My body has time. So do I.
**Top of head:** I am building something real, week by week.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Adapted from Day 2 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s.
